content.content:
author:	dlapiduz
association:	owner
edited:	false
status:	none
--
## Technical Design

### Approach
At platform startup, if `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, create a go-sdk MCP client, connect to the coach via `StreamableClientTransport`, enumerate all prompts and resources using `cs.Prompts()` and `cs.Resources()` iterators, and register per-item forwarding closures on the platform server. The go-sdk (v1.3.0) has no built-in proxy mode — enumeration + closure registration is the only viable approach. Bearer token auth on outbound requests via a custom `http.RoundTripper`. Graceful degradation: if coach is unreachable, log a warning and continue.

Full design doc: `docs/architecture/issue-58-coach-proxy.md`

### Changes Required
- New: `internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go` — `RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, server, coachURL, coachToken)`
- Modify: `internal/config/config.go` — add `CoachURL` and `CoachToken` fields (IAF_COACH_URL, IAF_COACH_TOKEN)
- Modify: `cmd/apiserver/main.go` — call `RegisterCoachProxy` if `cfg.CoachURL != ""`
- Modify: `internal/mcp/server.go` — update `serverInstructions` when coach is configured
- Modify: `docs/architecture.md` — component diagram: Agent → Platform MCP → Coach MCP

### Data / API Changes
New config fields: `CoachURL string` (IAF_COACH_URL), `CoachToken string` (IAF_COACH_TOKEN). Both default to "".

**Proxy mechanism:**
```go
// bearerRoundTripper injects auth token into every outbound request
// cs.Prompts(ctx, nil) — iterate all coach prompts
// server.AddPrompt(p, func() { return cs.GetPrompt(...) }) — register forwarding closure
// cs.Resources(ctx, nil) — iterate all coach resources
// server.AddResource(r, func() { return cs.ReadResource(...) }) — register forwarding closure
```

The `ClientSession` is shared for the platform lifetime (not per-agent). Coaching content is org-wide — sharing one connection is correct.

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact
Platform-to-coach is a single internal connection. Coach receives no tenant context — serves same standards to all. Coach ingress hostname must be distinct from platform hostname and app hostnames (system-level, not agent-deployed).

### Security Considerations
- `IAF_COACH_TOKEN`: mount from K8s Secret, never log its value.
- TLS: default Go TLS stack validates certificates. No custom config needed if cert-manager issues with in-cluster CA.
- Hot-reload not supported: platform restart required if `IAF_COACH_URL` changes. Documented limitation.
- The coach must not be directly reachable by external agents without auth.

### Resource & Performance Impact
One persistent HTTP connection. Forwarding adds one round-trip per coaching call (infrequent). Negligible memory overhead.

### Migration / Compatibility
No agent-visible change — prompt/resource names and URIs are preserved exactly. Rollout: deploy coach (#56) → set IAF_COACH_URL → restart platform. Until IAF_COACH_URL is set, platform serves local fallback coaching content.

### Open Questions for Developer
- **Go version check**: `cs.Prompts()` is a range-over-func iterator (Go 1.23+). Verify minimum Go version. If < 1.23, use `cs.ListPrompts()` with cursor pagination instead.
- Coach session reconnect strategy: `StreamableClientTransport` handles SSE reconnects but not MCP client session restart. Accept that platform restart is needed if coach goes down (document this).
- Should failed coach registration be fatal (return error) or always degrade? Recommendation: always degrade (return nil + warning log).

### Dependencies
Depends on #56 (Coach server).
--

content.content:
# Architecture: Platform MCP Proxy to Coach Server (Issue #58)

## Approach

When `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform MCP server connects to the coach as an MCP
client at startup, enumerates all coach prompts and resources, and registers forwarding
closures on the platform server. From an agent's perspective, the full set of prompts
and resources appears on the single platform endpoint — no agent-side configuration
changes needed.

The go-sdk `ClientSession` exposes `Prompts()` and `Resources()` iterators and
`GetPrompt()` / `ReadResource()` methods. The go-sdk has **no built-in proxy mode**;
the platform must enumerate at startup and register per-item forwarding closures. This
is the only viable approach with the current SDK version (1.3.0).

If `IAF_COACH_URL` is unset, the platform continues serving its local coaching content
(the fallback registrations from Issue #56). If the coach is unreachable at startup, the
platform logs a warning and continues with local coaching content — it does not fail.

## Changes Required

### New files

- `internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go` — `RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, server, coachURL, coachToken)` function

### Modified files

- `internal/config/config.go` — add `CoachURL string` and `CoachToken string` fields;
  map to `IAF_COACH_URL` / `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` env vars
- `cmd/apiserver/main.go` — after building the platform MCP server, call
  `iafmcp.RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, mcpServer, cfg.CoachURL, cfg.CoachToken)` if
  `cfg.CoachURL != ""`
- `internal/mcp/server.go` — update `serverInstructions` with a note about the coach
  when coach is configured (pass a `coachConfigured bool` param, or detect dynamically)
- `docs/architecture.md` — add component diagram: Agent → Platform MCP → Coach MCP

## Data / API Changes

### New config fields

```go
// internal/config/config.go additions
CoachURL   string `mapstructure:"coach_url"`   // IAF_COACH_URL
CoachToken string `mapstructure:"coach_token"` // IAF_COACH_TOKEN
```

Defaults: both empty strings (feature disabled).

### `RegisterCoachProxy` implementation outline

```go
// internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go
package mcp

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "net/http"

    gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
)

// bearerRoundTripper injects a Bearer token into every request.
type bearerRoundTripper struct {
    token string
    base  http.RoundTripper
}

func (b *bearerRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
    req = req.Clone(req.Context())
    req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+b.token)
    return b.base.RoundTrip(req)
}

// RegisterCoachProxy connects to the coach MCP server and registers forwarding
// handlers for every prompt and resource the coach exposes. Safe to call
// concurrently with server startup; registers before the server accepts connections.
//
// If the coach is unreachable, RegisterCoachProxy logs a warning and returns nil
// (platform continues serving without coaching content from the remote coach).
func RegisterCoachProxy(ctx context.Context, server *gomcp.Server, coachURL, coachToken string) error {
    httpClient := &http.Client{
        Transport: &bearerRoundTripper{
            token: coachToken,
            base:  http.DefaultTransport,
        },
    }

    client := gomcp.NewClient(
        &gomcp.Implementation{Name: "iaf-platform-proxy", Version: "0.1.0"},
        nil,
    )
    cs, err := client.Connect(ctx, &gomcp.StreamableClientTransport{
        Endpoint:   coachURL,
        HTTPClient: httpClient,
    }, nil)
    if err != nil {
        slog.Warn("coach proxy: could not connect to coach; coaching prompts/resources unavailable", "url", coachURL, "error", err)
        return nil // graceful degradation — not a fatal error
    }

    // Enumerate and forward prompts.
    for p, err := range cs.Prompts(ctx, nil) {
        if err != nil {
            slog.Warn("coach proxy: error iterating prompts", "error", err)
            break
        }
        prompt := p // capture loop var
        server.AddPrompt(prompt, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.GetPromptRequest) (*gomcp.GetPromptResult, error) {
            return cs.GetPrompt(ctx, &gomcp.GetPromptParams{
                Name:      req.Params.Name,
                Arguments: req.Params.Arguments,
            })
        })
    }

    // Enumerate and forward resources.
    for r, err := range cs.Resources(ctx, nil) {
        if err != nil {
            slog.Warn("coach proxy: error iterating resources", "error", err)
            break
        }
        resource := r // capture loop var
        server.AddResource(resource, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.ReadResourceRequest) (*gomcp.ReadResourceResult, error) {
            return cs.ReadResource(ctx, &gomcp.ReadResourceParams{URI: req.Params.URI})
        })
    }

    slog.Info("coach proxy: registered coaching prompts and resources", "url", coachURL)
    return nil
}
```

The `cs` (ClientSession) must remain open for the lifetime of the platform server.
Since the forwarding closures capture `cs`, the session stays live as long as the
platform process runs. The `ctx` passed to `RegisterCoachProxy` should be the platform's
root context (cancelled on shutdown).

### Auth: Bearer token on outbound requests

The platform injects `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` via `bearerRoundTripper`. The coach validates
this token using its standard Echo `middleware.Auth` middleware. No other credential
exchange is needed.

## Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact

- The proxy connection is a single shared `ClientSession` from the platform process to
  the coach — not per-agent or per-session.
- Coaching prompts and resources are stateless and org-wide; sharing a single connection
  is correct.
- The coach receives no tenant/namespace context — it serves the same standards to all
  callers. This is intentional.
- Ingress hostname for the coach (`coach.iaf.localhost` by default) must be distinct
  from the platform hostname (`iaf.localhost`). No hostname collision with app names
  because the coach is a fixed system-level deployment, not an agent-deployed app.

## Security Considerations

- **Cluster-internal traffic**: the coach should be deployed in `iaf-system` namespace
  and its ingress host should not be exposed externally (or should require the same auth
  token). The platform-to-coach connection uses `IAF_COACH_TOKEN`; external agents
  cannot bypass this by connecting directly to the coach without knowing the token.
- **Token handling**: `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` must be treated as a secret (mount from K8s
  Secret, not ConfigMap). Never log the token value. The `bearerRoundTripper` adds it
  only to outbound headers — it is not visible in proxy responses.
- **TLS**: if the coach ingress uses TLS (recommended for production), the
  `StreamableClientTransport.HTTPClient` should use the default Go TLS stack, which
  validates certificates. No custom TLS config needed for cluster-internal self-signed
  certs if cert-manager issues them with the in-cluster CA.
- **Prompt/resource enumeration at startup only**: the platform does not re-enumerate
  coach capabilities at runtime. If new prompts/resources are added to the coach, a
  platform restart is required. This is documented as a known limitation.

## Resource & Performance Impact

- One persistent HTTP connection from the platform to the coach (SSE stream for server-
  initiated notifications — the SDK opens this automatically via `StreamableClientTransport`)
- Forwarding adds one HTTP round-trip per prompt/resource call. Latency budget: coaching
  calls are not on hot paths (humans/agents call them infrequently).
- Memory overhead is negligible (forwarding closures are tiny).

## Migration / Compatibility

- **Rollout order**: deploy coach (Issue #56) → set `IAF_COACH_URL` on platform → platform
  proxy registration happens at next restart. Until `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform
  serves its local fallback coaching content.
- **Hot-reload**: not supported. Changing `IAF_COACH_URL` requires a platform restart.
  This is documented as a known limitation.
- **No agent-visible change**: prompt and resource names/URIs are preserved exactly as
  the coach exposes them. Agents see no difference.

## Open Questions for Developer

- The `cs.Prompts()` iterator (Go 1.23 range-over-func) requires Go 1.23+. Verify the
  project's minimum Go version. If < 1.23, use `cs.ListPrompts()` with pagination
  instead of the iterator form.
- Decide whether failed coach registration should block startup (return error) or
  always degrade gracefully. Recommendation: always degrade (return nil) with a warning
  log, since the platform is still fully functional without coaching.
- The coach `ClientSession` should have a keepalive or reconnect strategy if the coach
  restarts. The `StreamableClientTransport` handles reconnects for the SSE stream, but
  not for the MCP client session itself. Consider wrapping in a retry loop or accepting
  the platform needs a restart if the coach goes down.

toolUseResult.stdout:
# Architecture: Platform MCP Proxy to Coach Server (Issue #58)

## Approach

When `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform MCP server connects to the coach as an MCP
client at startup, enumerates all coach prompts and resources, and registers forwarding
closures on the platform server. From an agent's perspective, the full set of prompts
and resources appears on the single platform endpoint — no agent-side configuration
changes needed.

The go-sdk `ClientSession` exposes `Prompts()` and `Resources()` iterators and
`GetPrompt()` / `ReadResource()` methods. The go-sdk has **no built-in proxy mode**;
the platform must enumerate at startup and register per-item forwarding closures. This
is the only viable approach with the current SDK version (1.3.0).

If `IAF_COACH_URL` is unset, the platform continues serving its local coaching content
(the fallback registrations from Issue #56). If the coach is unreachable at startup, the
platform logs a warning and continues with local coaching content — it does not fail.

## Changes Required

### New files

- `internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go` — `RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, server, coachURL, coachToken)` function

### Modified files

- `internal/config/config.go` — add `CoachURL string` and `CoachToken string` fields;
  map to `IAF_COACH_URL` / `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` env vars
- `cmd/apiserver/main.go` — after building the platform MCP server, call
  `iafmcp.RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, mcpServer, cfg.CoachURL, cfg.CoachToken)` if
  `cfg.CoachURL != ""`
- `internal/mcp/server.go` — update `serverInstructions` with a note about the coach
  when coach is configured (pass a `coachConfigured bool` param, or detect dynamically)
- `docs/architecture.md` — add component diagram: Agent → Platform MCP → Coach MCP

## Data / API Changes

### New config fields

```go
// internal/config/config.go additions
CoachURL   string `mapstructure:"coach_url"`   // IAF_COACH_URL
CoachToken string `mapstructure:"coach_token"` // IAF_COACH_TOKEN
```

Defaults: both empty strings (feature disabled).

### `RegisterCoachProxy` implementation outline

```go
// internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go
package mcp

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "net/http"

    gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
)

// bearerRoundTripper injects a Bearer token into every request.
type bearerRoundTripper struct {
    token string
    base  http.RoundTripper
}

func (b *bearerRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
    req = req.Clone(req.Context())
    req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+b.token)
    return b.base.RoundTrip(req)
}

// RegisterCoachProxy connects to the coach MCP server and registers forwarding
// handlers for every prompt and resource the coach exposes. Safe to call
// concurrently with server startup; registers before the server accepts connections.
//
// If the coach is unreachable, RegisterCoachProxy logs a warning and returns nil
// (platform continues serving without coaching content from the remote coach).
func RegisterCoachProxy(ctx context.Context, server *gomcp.Server, coachURL, coachToken string) error {
    httpClient := &http.Client{
        Transport: &bearerRoundTripper{
            token: coachToken,
            base:  http.DefaultTransport,
        },
    }

    client := gomcp.NewClient(
        &gomcp.Implementation{Name: "iaf-platform-proxy", Version: "0.1.0"},
        nil,
    )
    cs, err := client.Connect(ctx, &gomcp.StreamableClientTransport{
        Endpoint:   coachURL,
        HTTPClient: httpClient,
    }, nil)
    if err != nil {
        slog.Warn("coach proxy: could not connect to coach; coaching prompts/resources unavailable", "url", coachURL, "error", err)
        return nil // graceful degradation — not a fatal error
    }

    // Enumerate and forward prompts.
    for p, err := range cs.Prompts(ctx, nil) {
        if err != nil {
            slog.Warn("coach proxy: error iterating prompts", "error", err)
            break
        }
        prompt := p // capture loop var
        server.AddPrompt(prompt, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.GetPromptRequest) (*gomcp.GetPromptResult, error) {
            return cs.GetPrompt(ctx, &gomcp.GetPromptParams{
                Name:      req.Params.Name,
                Arguments: req.Params.Arguments,
            })
        })
    }

    // Enumerate and forward resources.
    for r, err := range cs.Resources(ctx, nil) {
        if err != nil {
            slog.Warn("coach proxy: error iterating resources", "error", err)
            break
        }
        resource := r // capture loop var
        server.AddResource(resource, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.ReadResourceRequest) (*gomcp.ReadResourceResult, error) {
            return cs.ReadResource(ctx, &gomcp.ReadResourceParams{URI: req.Params.URI})
        })
    }

    slog.Info("coach proxy: registered coaching prompts and resources", "url", coachURL)
    return nil
}
```

The `cs` (ClientSession) must remain open for the lifetime of the platform server.
Since the forwarding closures capture `cs`, the session stays live as long as the
platform process runs. The `ctx` passed to `RegisterCoachProxy` should be the platform's
root context (cancelled on shutdown).

### Auth: Bearer token on outbound requests

The platform injects `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` via `bearerRoundTripper`. The coach validates
this token using its standard Echo `middleware.Auth` middleware. No other credential
exchange is needed.

## Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact

- The proxy connection is a single shared `ClientSession` from the platform process to
  the coach — not per-agent or per-session.
- Coaching prompts and resources are stateless and org-wide; sharing a single connection
  is correct.
- The coach receives no tenant/namespace context — it serves the same standards to all
  callers. This is intentional.
- Ingress hostname for the coach (`coach.iaf.localhost` by default) must be distinct
  from the platform hostname (`iaf.localhost`). No hostname collision with app names
  because the coach is a fixed system-level deployment, not an agent-deployed app.

## Security Considerations

- **Cluster-internal traffic**: the coach should be deployed in `iaf-system` namespace
  and its ingress host should not be exposed externally (or should require the same auth
  token). The platform-to-coach connection uses `IAF_COACH_TOKEN`; external agents
  cannot bypass this by connecting directly to the coach without knowing the token.
- **Token handling**: `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` must be treated as a secret (mount from K8s
  Secret, not ConfigMap). Never log the token value. The `bearerRoundTripper` adds it
  only to outbound headers — it is not visible in proxy responses.
- **TLS**: if the coach ingress uses TLS (recommended for production), the
  `StreamableClientTransport.HTTPClient` should use the default Go TLS stack, which
  validates certificates. No custom TLS config needed for cluster-internal self-signed
  certs if cert-manager issues them with the in-cluster CA.
- **Prompt/resource enumeration at startup only**: the platform does not re-enumerate
  coach capabilities at runtime. If new prompts/resources are added to the coach, a
  platform restart is required. This is documented as a known limitation.

## Resource & Performance Impact

- One persistent HTTP connection from the platform to the coach (SSE stream for server-
  initiated notifications — the SDK opens this automatically via `StreamableClientTransport`)
- Forwarding adds one HTTP round-trip per prompt/resource call. Latency budget: coaching
  calls are not on hot paths (humans/agents call them infrequently).
- Memory overhead is negligible (forwarding closures are tiny).

## Migration / Compatibility

- **Rollout order**: deploy coach (Issue #56) → set `IAF_COACH_URL` on platform → platform
  proxy registration happens at next restart. Until `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform
  serves its local fallback coaching content.
- **Hot-reload**: not supported. Changing `IAF_COACH_URL` requires a platform restart.
  This is documented as a known limitation.
- **No agent-visible change**: prompt and resource names/URIs are preserved exactly as
  the coach exposes them. Agents see no difference.

## Open Questions for Developer

- The `cs.Prompts()` iterator (Go 1.23 range-over-func) requires Go 1.23+. Verify the
  project's minimum Go version. If < 1.23, use `cs.ListPrompts()` with pagination
  instead of the iterator form.
- Decide whether failed coach registration should block startup (return error) or
  always degrade gracefully. Recommendation: always degrade (return nil) with a warning
  log, since the platform is still fully functional without coaching.
- The coach `ClientSession` should have a keepalive or reconnect strategy if the coach
  restarts. The `StreamableClientTransport` handles reconnects for the SSE stream, but
  not for the MCP client session itself. Consider wrapping in a retry loop or accepting
  the platform needs a restart if the coach goes down.

git add internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go internal/mcp/coach_proxy_test.go internal/config/config.go cmd/apiserver/main.go internal/api/handlers/applications_test.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(proxy): proxy coaching requests from platform MCP to coach server (closes #58)

When IAF_COACH_URL is set, RegisterCoachProxy connects to the coach at startup,
enumerates all its prompts and resources, and registers forwarding closures on the
platform MCP server. Agents see coaching content transparently on the platform
endpoint with no client-side changes required.

- internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go: RegisterCoachProxy + bearerRoundTripper
- internal/config/config.go: IAF_COACH_URL / IAF_COACH_TOKEN fields
- cmd/apiserver/main.go: call RegisterCoachProxy when CoachURL is set
- Graceful degradation: unreachable coach logs a warning and continues
- Fix: applications_test.go Register() call updated to match new TTL signature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(proxy): proxy coaching requests from platform MCP to coach server" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- New `internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go` — `RegisterCoachProxy(ctx, server, coachURL, coachToken)` connects to the coach at startup, enumerates all its prompts/resources, and registers forwarding closures on the platform server
- `bearerRoundTripper` injects `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` into every outbound request (token never logged)
- `internal/config/config.go` — new `IAF_COACH_URL` and `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` fields
- `cmd/apiserver/main.go` — calls `RegisterCoachProxy` when `CoachURL != ""`
- Graceful degradation: unreachable coach logs a warning and continues with local fallback coaching content
- Bugfix: `applications_test.go` updated to match the `Register(name, ttl)` signature added in issue #6

## Rollout
1. Deploy coach server (#56)
2. Set `IAF_COACH_URL` + `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` on the platform
3. Restart platform — coaching prompts/resources now proxied transparently

## Known limitations (documented in design doc)
- Platform restart required if coach goes down or `IAF_COACH_URL` changes (no hot-reload)
- Coach capabilities are enumerated once at startup; new coach prompts/resources require a platform restart

## Test plan
- [x] `TestRegisterCoachProxy_Unreachable` — graceful degradation, returns nil
- [x] `TestRegisterCoachProxy_ForwardsPrompts` — real in-process coach, 6 prompts proxied
- [x] `TestRegisterCoachProxy_ForwardsResources` — 4 resources proxied; bearer token validated
- [x] `make test` — all packages green

Closes #58

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

PR up: https://github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/pull/68

What was implemented:

- **`internal/mcp/coach_proxy.go`** — `RegisterCoachProxy` + `bearerRoundTripper`. Uses Go 1.23 range-over-func iterators (`cs.Prompts()` / `cs.Resources()`) to enumerate the coach at startup and register forwarding closures. Always degrades gracefully on connection failure.
- **`internal/config/config.go`** — `IAF_COACH_URL` and `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` fields (both default empty = feature disabled)
- **`cmd/apiserver/main.go`** — calls proxy registration after building the platform MCP server, guarded by `cfg.CoachURL != ""`
- **Bugfix** — `applications_test.go` `Register()` call updated to match the TTL signature from issue #6

The tests actually spin up a real in-process coach HTTP server and verify that prompts/resources are correctly forwarded to the platform server. Ready for #59 next?

input.description: Smoke test health and MCP endpoint

content.content:
{"status":"ok"}

event: message
data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"capabilities":{"logging":{},"prompts":{"listChanged":true},"resources":{"listChanged":true},"tools":{"listChanged":true}},"instructions":"You are connected to the IAF (Intelligent Application Fabric) MCP server — a platform for deploying applications on Kubernetes.

CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Before calling any other tool, you MUST call the "register" tool to get a session_id. Every other tool requires session_id as a parameter. Without it, all tool calls will fail.

QUICK START:
1. Call "register" (with an optional friendly name) → returns a session_id
2. Call "push_code" with your session_id, an app name, and a map of file paths to contents → builds and deploys your code
3. Call "app_status" with your session_id and app name → check build/deploy progress
4. Once status is "Running", your app is live at http://<app-name>.<base-domain>

AVAILABLE TOOLS (all require session_id except register):
- register: Get a session_id (CALL THIS FIRST)
- unregister: Clean up session and all its resources when you are done (irreversible)
- push_code: Upload source code files to build and deploy (provide files as {"path": "content"} map)
- deploy_app: Deploy from a container image or git repo (use git_credential for private repos)
- list_apps: See all your deployed apps
- app_status: Check build/deploy progress for an app
- app_logs: View application or build logs
- delete_app: Remove an app and its resources
- add_git_credential: Store a git credential (username/password or SSH key) for private repo access
- list_git_credentials: List stored git credentials (no secrets returned)
- delete_git_credential: Remove a git credential
- list_data_sources: List all platform data sources (databases, APIs, etc.)
- get_data_source: Get details about a specific data source including env var names
- attach_data_source: Attach a data source to your app (injects credentials as env vars)
- provision_service: Provision a managed backing service (e.g. PostgreSQL) — poll service_status every 10s until Ready
- service_status: Check provisioning status; returns connectionEnvVars when Ready
- bind_service: Inject service credentials into an app as K8s Secret references
- unbind_service: Remove service credentials from an app
- deprovision_service: Delete a managed service (must unbind all apps first)
- list_services: List all managed services in your namespace

KEY DETAILS:
- Apps are built automatically using Cloud Native Buildpacks (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Ruby)
- Default port is 8080 — your app must listen on this port

content.thinking:
The user is showing me logs from an agent using IAF. The key observation from these logs is:

1. The agent is polling `app_status` very aggressively - it's calling it repeatedly in a tight loop without waiting between calls. It called `app_status` roughly 25+ times while waiting for the build to complete.

2. The `serverInstructions` tells the agent to "poll service_status every 10s until Ready" but doesn't give clear guidance on how often to poll `app_status` during builds.

3. The agent has no backoff - it's just hammering the endpoint. This is a problem because:
   - It wastes tokens/API calls for the agent
   - It puts unnecessary load on the platform
   - The instructions don't tell it to wait between polls

The fix should be in the `serverInstructions` in `internal/mcp/server.go` - I need to add explicit polling intervals like "check app_status every 30s during builds" and ideally the `app_status` response should include a `retryAfterSeconds` or `pollIntervalHint` field to guide the agent's behavior. Right now the instructions just say "Use app_status to monitor builds (typically ~2 minutes)" without specifying the polling cadence, whereas the service provisioning instructions are much more explicit about timing.

The real issue is that the agent is hammering the endpoint as fast as possible because nothing tells it to wait. I should add a `pollIntervalSeconds` field to the `app_status` response when the build is in progress, so the agent knows exactly how often to check back.

content.text:
The problem is clear: the agent is hammering `app_status` in a tight loop — roughly 25+ calls with no delay. Two causes:

1. **`serverInstructions` doesn't specify a poll interval for builds** — it says "typically ~2 minutes" but gives no guidance on how often to check. Compare with the managed services instruction which explicitly says "poll every 10s".

2. **`app_status` response doesn't include a backoff hint** — the agent has no machine-readable signal telling it to wait.

The simplest fix is both: add explicit poll guidance to the instructions, and add a `pollIntervalSeconds` field to `app_status` responses when the app is still building/deploying. Let me implement it:

1→package tools
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"net/url"
     8→
     9→	iafv1alpha1 "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/api/v1alpha1"
    10→	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/validation"
    11→	gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
    12→	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
    13→	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
    14→)
    15→
    16→type AppStatusInput struct {
    17→	SessionID string `json:"session_id" jsonschema:"required - session ID returned by the register tool"`
    18→	Name      string `json:"name" jsonschema:"required - application name to check status for"`
    19→}
    20→
    21→func RegisterAppStatus(server *gomcp.Server, deps *Dependencies) {
    22→	gomcp.AddTool(server, &gomcp.Tool{
    23→		Name:        "app_status",
    24→		Description: "Check the current status of an application — phase (Pending/Building/Deploying/Running/Failed), URL, build progress, and replica count. Requires session_id from the register tool and the application name. Use this after push_code or deploy_app to monitor progress.",
    25→	}, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.CallToolRequest, input AppStatusInput) (*gomcp.CallToolResult, any, error) {
    26→		namespace, err := deps.ResolveNamespace(input.SessionID)
    27→		if err != nil {
    28→			return nil, nil, err
    29→		}
    30→		if err := validation.ValidateAppName(input.Name); err != nil {
    31→			return nil, nil, err
    32→		}
    33→
    34→		var app iafv1alpha1.Application
    35→		if err := deps.Client.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: input.Name, Namespace: namespace}, &app); err != nil {
    36→			if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
    37→				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("application %q not found", input.Name)
    38→			}
    39→			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("getting application: %w", err)
    40→		}
    41→
    42→		result := map[string]any{
    43→			"name":              app.Name,
    44→			"phase":             string(app.Status.Phase),
    45→			"url":               app.Status.URL,
    46→			"latestImage":       app.Status.LatestImage,
    47→			"buildStatus":       app.Status.BuildStatus,
    48→			"availableReplicas": app.Status.AvailableReplicas,
    49→			"replicas":          app.Spec.Replicas,
    50→			"port":              app.Spec.Port,
    51→		}
    52→
    53→		// Add source info
    54→		if app.Spec.Image != "" {
    55→			result["sourceType"] = "image"
    56→			result["image"] = app.Spec.Image
    57→		} else if app.Spec.Git != nil {
    58→			result["sourceType"] = "git"
    59→			result["gitUrl"] = app.Spec.Git.URL
    60→			result["gitRevision"] = app.Spec.Git.Revision
    61→		} else if app.Spec.Blob != "" {
    62→			result["sourceType"] = "code"
    63→		}
    64→
    65→		if len(app.Status.Conditions) > 0 {
    66→			conditions := make([]map[string]string, 0, len(app.Status.Conditions))
    67→			for _, c := range app.Status.Conditions {
    68→				conditions = append(conditions, map[string]string{
    69→					"type":    c.Type,
    70→					"status":  string(c.Status),
    71→					"reason":  c.Reason,
    72→					"message": c.Message,
    73→				})
    74→			}
    75→			result["conditions"] = conditions
    76→		}
    77→
    78→		// Add Grafana Explore deep link when Tempo is configured.
    79→		if deps.TempoURL != "" {
    80→			result["traceExploreUrl"] = buildTraceExploreURL(deps.TempoURL, app.Name)
    81→		}
    82→
    83→		text, _ := json.MarshalIndent(result, "", "  ")
    84→		return &gomcp.CallToolResult{
    85→			Content: []gomcp.Content{&gomcp.TextContent{Text: string(text)}},
    86→		}, nil, nil
    87→	})
    88→}
    89→
    90→// buildTraceExploreURL constructs a Grafana Explore deep link pre-filtered to
    91→// the given application's service.name using TraceQL. The grafanaURL comes from
    92→// platform config (IAF_TEMPO_URL), never from agent input.
    93→func buildTraceExploreURL(grafanaURL, appName string) string {
    94→	// TraceQL query scoped to this app's service.name.
    95→	query := fmt.Sprintf(`{service.name="%s"}`, appName)
    96→	left := fmt.Sprintf(`{"datasource":"Tempo","queries":[{"query":%s,"queryType":"traceql"}],"range":{"from":"now-1h","to":"now"}}`,
    97→		string(mustMarshalJSON(query)))
    98→	params := url.Values{}
    99→	params.Set("orgId", "1")
   100→	params.Set("left", left)
   101→	return grafanaURL + "/explore?" + params.Encode()
   102→}
   103→
   104→func mustMarshalJSON(v any) []byte {
   105→	b, _ := json.Marshal(v)
   106→	return b
   107→}
   108→

package mcp

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/auth"
	iafgithub "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/github"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/prompts"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/resources"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/tools"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/orgstandards"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/sourcestore"
	gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
)

// serverInstructions is sent to MCP clients during initialization and injected
// into the LLM's system prompt. This is the primary way agents learn how to
// use IAF.
const serverInstructions = `You are connected to the IAF (Intelligent Application Fabric) MCP server — a platform for deploying applications on Kubernetes.

CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Before calling any other tool, you MUST call the "register" tool to get a session_id. Every other tool requires session_id as a parameter. Without it, all tool calls will fail.

QUICK START:
1. Call "register" (with an optional friendly name) → returns a session_id
2. Call "push_code" with your session_id, an app name, and a map of file paths to contents → builds and deploys your code
3. Call "app_status" with your session_id and app name → check build/deploy progress
4. Once status is "Running", your app is live at http://<app-name>.<base-domain>

AVAILABLE TOOLS (all require session_id except register):
- register: Get a session_id (CALL THIS FIRST)
- unregister: Clean up session and all its resources when you are done (irreversible)
- push_code: Upload source code files to build and deploy (provide files as {"path": "content"} map)
- deploy_app: Deploy from a container image or git repo (use git_credential for private repos)
- list_apps: See all your deployed apps
- app_status: Check build/deploy progress for an app
- app_logs: View application or build logs
- delete_app: Remove an app and its resources
- add_git_credential: Store a git credential (username/password or SSH key) for private repo access
- list_git_credentials: List stored git credentials (no secrets returned)
- delete_git_credential: Remove a git credential
- list_data_sources: List all platform data sources (databases, APIs, etc.)
- get_data_source: Get details about a specific data source including env var names
- attach_data_source: Attach a data source to your app (injects credentials as env vars)
- provision_service: Provision a managed backing service (e.g. PostgreSQL) — poll service_status every 10s until Ready
- service_status: Check provisioning status; returns connectionEnvVars when Ready
- bind_service: Inject service credentials into an app as K8s Secret references
- unbind_service: Remove service credentials from an app
- deprovision_service: Delete a managed service (must unbind all apps first)
- list_services: List all managed services in your namespace

KEY DETAILS:
- Apps are built automatically using Cloud Native Buildpacks (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Ruby)
- Default port is 8080 — your app must listen on this port
- Your app will be available at http://<app-name>.<base-domain> once Running
- Each session gets its own isolated Kubernetes namespace
- Use app_status to monitor builds (typically ~2 minutes)
- Use app_logs with build_logs=true to debug build failures

CODING STANDARDS:
- Read the coding-guide prompt for organisation coding standards before writing any code
- Read iaf://org/coding-standards for the machine-readable standards document`

// NewServer creates and configures the MCP server with all tools.
// If loader is non-nil, org standards are served from that loader; otherwise platform defaults are used.
// ghClient may be nil — GitHub tools are omitted when it is not set.
// If clientset is non-nil, app_logs will stream real logs from pods.
// sessionTTL sets the idle TTL for new sessions (0 = no expiry).
func NewServer(k8sClient client.Client, sessions *auth.SessionStore, store *sourcestore.Store, baseDomain string, loader *orgstandards.Loader, ghClient iafgithub.Client, ghOrg, ghToken string, tempoURL string, sessionTTL time.Duration, clientset ...kubernetes.Interface) *gomcp.Server {
	server := gomcp.NewServer(
		&gomcp.Implementation{
			Name:    "iaf",
			Version: "0.1.0",
		},
		&gomcp.ServerOptions{
			Instructions: serverInstructions,
		},
	)

	deps := &tools.Dependencies{
		Client:       k8sClient,
		Store:        store,
		BaseDomain:   baseDomain,
		Sessions:     sessions,
		OrgStandards: loader,
		GitHub:       ghClient,
		GitHubOrg:    ghOrg,
		GitHubToken:  ghToken,
		TempoURL:     tempoURL,
		SessionTTL:   sessionTTL,
	}

	tools.RegisterRegisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterUnregisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeployApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterPushCode(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAddGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListGitCredentials(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAppStatus(server, deps)
	if len(clientset) > 0 && clientset[0] != nil {
		tools.RegisterAppLogsWithClientset(server, deps, clientset[0])
	} else {
		tools.RegisterAppLogs(server, deps)
	}
	tools.RegisterListApps(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListDataSources(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterGetDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAttachDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterProvisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterServiceStatus(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterBindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterUnbindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeprovisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListServices(server, deps)

	prompts.RegisterDeployGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLanguageGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterCodingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterScaffoldGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterServicesGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLoggingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterMetricsGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterTracingGuide(server, deps)

	resources.RegisterPlatformInfo(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLanguageResources(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterApplicationSpec(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterOrgStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterScaffoldResource(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterDataCatalog(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLoggingStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterMetricsStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterTracingStandards(server, deps)

	// GitHub components — registered only when a token and org are configured.
	if deps.GitHub != nil {
		tools.RegisterSetupGithubRepo(server, deps)
		resources.RegisterGitHubStandards(server, deps)
		prompts.RegisterGitHubGuide(server, deps)
	}

	return server
}

-- Use app_status to monitor builds (typically ~2 minutes)

- Use app_logs with build_logs=true to debug build failures

fix(status): add pollIntervalSeconds hint to prevent agent busy-polling

Agents were calling app_status in a tight loop (~25+ calls) while waiting
for builds to complete. Fixed with two changes:

- app_status response now includes "pollIntervalSeconds" (30 during Building,
  15 during Deploying, absent when terminal) so agents have a machine-readable
  backoff signal to respect
- serverInstructions updated to explicitly state the wait intervals and call
  out the field, using WAIT/MUST language to be unambiguous

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- Read the coding-guide prompt for organisation coding standards before writing any code
- Read iaf://org/coding-standards for the machine-readable standards document

IMPORTANT RULES (follow for every app):
1. Write all code to disk before uploading — do not pass code as strings in memory, write actual files first
2. Use version control if the setup_github_repo tool is available — commit code before deploying
3. No in-memory storage (arrays, maps, global variables for data) — apps restart and lose state; use a managed database instead (provision_service for PostgreSQL, or attach a data source)
4. Follow 12-factor app principles: config via env vars, stateless processes, explicit dependencies, stdout logging
5. Authentication on admin/sensitive routes is encouraged but NOT required on read-only public endpoints`

package mcp

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/auth"
	iafgithub "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/github"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/prompts"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/resources"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/tools"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/orgstandards"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/sourcestore"
	gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
)

// serverInstructions is sent to MCP clients during initialization and injected
// into the LLM's system prompt. This is the primary way agents learn how to
// use IAF.
const serverInstructions = `You are connected to the IAF (Intelligent Application Fabric) MCP server — a platform for deploying applications on Kubernetes.

CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Before calling any other tool, you MUST call the "register" tool to get a session_id. Every other tool requires session_id as a parameter. Without it, all tool calls will fail.

QUICK START:
1. Call "register" (with an optional friendly name) → returns a session_id
2. Call "push_code" with your session_id, an app name, and a map of file paths to contents → builds and deploys your code
3. Call "app_status" with your session_id and app name → check build/deploy progress
4. Once status is "Running", your app is live at http://<app-name>.<base-domain>

AVAILABLE TOOLS (all require session_id except register):
- register: Get a session_id (CALL THIS FIRST)
- unregister: Clean up session and all its resources when you are done (irreversible)
- push_code: Upload source code files to build and deploy (provide files as {"path": "content"} map)
- deploy_app: Deploy from a container image or git repo (use git_credential for private repos)
- list_apps: See all your deployed apps
- app_status: Check build/deploy progress for an app
- app_logs: View application or build logs
- delete_app: Remove an app and its resources
- add_git_credential: Store a git credential (username/password or SSH key) for private repo access
- list_git_credentials: List stored git credentials (no secrets returned)
- delete_git_credential: Remove a git credential
- list_data_sources: List all platform data sources (databases, APIs, etc.)
- get_data_source: Get details about a specific data source including env var names
- attach_data_source: Attach a data source to your app (injects credentials as env vars)
- provision_service: Provision a managed backing service (e.g. PostgreSQL) — poll service_status every 10s until Ready
- service_status: Check provisioning status; returns connectionEnvVars when Ready
- bind_service: Inject service credentials into an app as K8s Secret references
- unbind_service: Remove service credentials from an app
- deprovision_service: Delete a managed service (must unbind all apps first)
- list_services: List all managed services in your namespace

KEY DETAILS:
- Apps are built automatically using Cloud Native Buildpacks (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Ruby)
- Default port is 8080 — your app must listen on this port
- Your app will be available at http://<app-name>.<base-domain> once Running
- Each session gets its own isolated Kubernetes namespace
- Use app_status to monitor builds — WAIT 30 seconds between each poll during Building, 15 seconds during Deploying. The response includes a "pollIntervalSeconds" field — always respect it. Builds typically take ~2 minutes; do not poll faster than the hint.
- Use app_logs with build_logs=true to debug build failures

CODING STANDARDS:
- Read the coding-guide prompt for organisation coding standards before writing any code
- Read iaf://org/coding-standards for the machine-readable standards document`

// NewServer creates and configures the MCP server with all tools.
// If loader is non-nil, org standards are served from that loader; otherwise platform defaults are used.
// ghClient may be nil — GitHub tools are omitted when it is not set.
// If clientset is non-nil, app_logs will stream real logs from pods.
// sessionTTL sets the idle TTL for new sessions (0 = no expiry).
func NewServer(k8sClient client.Client, sessions *auth.SessionStore, store *sourcestore.Store, baseDomain string, loader *orgstandards.Loader, ghClient iafgithub.Client, ghOrg, ghToken string, tempoURL string, sessionTTL time.Duration, clientset ...kubernetes.Interface) *gomcp.Server {
	server := gomcp.NewServer(
		&gomcp.Implementation{
			Name:    "iaf",
			Version: "0.1.0",
		},
		&gomcp.ServerOptions{
			Instructions: serverInstructions,
		},
	)

	deps := &tools.Dependencies{
		Client:       k8sClient,
		Store:        store,
		BaseDomain:   baseDomain,
		Sessions:     sessions,
		OrgStandards: loader,
		GitHub:       ghClient,
		GitHubOrg:    ghOrg,
		GitHubToken:  ghToken,
		TempoURL:     tempoURL,
		SessionTTL:   sessionTTL,
	}

	tools.RegisterRegisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterUnregisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeployApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterPushCode(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAddGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListGitCredentials(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAppStatus(server, deps)
	if len(clientset) > 0 && clientset[0] != nil {
		tools.RegisterAppLogsWithClientset(server, deps, clientset[0])
	} else {
		tools.RegisterAppLogs(server, deps)
	}
	tools.RegisterListApps(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListDataSources(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterGetDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAttachDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterProvisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterServiceStatus(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterBindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterUnbindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeprovisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListServices(server, deps)

	prompts.RegisterDeployGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLanguageGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterCodingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterScaffoldGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterServicesGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLoggingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterMetricsGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterTracingGuide(server, deps)

	resources.RegisterPlatformInfo(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLanguageResources(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterApplicationSpec(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterOrgStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterScaffoldResource(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterDataCatalog(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLoggingStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterMetricsStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterTracingStandards(server, deps)

	// GitHub components — registered only when a token and org are configured.
	if deps.GitHub != nil {
		tools.RegisterSetupGithubRepo(server, deps)
		resources.RegisterGitHubStandards(server, deps)
		prompts.RegisterGitHubGuide(server, deps)
	}

	return server
}

## Full Standards Reference
")
		sb.WriteString("Read `iaf://org/coding-standards` for the machine-readable JSON version of these standards.
")
input.new_string:
sb.WriteString("\n## IAF Platform Requirements\n")
		sb.WriteString(`These rules are mandatory for all apps deployed on IAF:

**1. Write code to disk first**
Always write your source files to the local filesystem before calling push_code.
Do not pass code as inline strings. Agents should create a project directory, write all files, then upload.

**2. No in-memory state**
IAF apps are ephemeral — pods restart and lose all in-memory data (arrays, maps, global variables).
Use a persistent backing service instead:
- Call ` + "`provision_service`" + ` to create a managed PostgreSQL database
- Call ` + "`list_data_sources`" + ` to find pre-provisioned databases or APIs
- Call ` + "`attach_data_source`" + ` / ` + "`bind_service`" + ` to inject credentials as env vars

**3. 12-factor app principles**
- Config from env vars only (never hardcoded)
- Stateless processes — no local filesystem state between requests
- Explicit dependency manifests (package.json, go.mod, requirements.txt, Gemfile)
- Log to stdout in structured JSON (see logging-guide)

**4. Version control**
If ` + "`setup_github_repo`" + ` is available, create a repo and commit your code before deploying.
This provides a history, enables collaboration, and makes rollbacks possible.

**5. Authentication**
Required on any route that modifies data or accesses sensitive information.
Optional on read-only public endpoints — do not add unnecessary auth to health checks or public views.
`)

		sb.WriteString("\n## Full Standards Reference\n")
		sb.WriteString("Read `iaf://org/coding-standards` for the machine-readable JSON version of these standards.\n")

fix(standards): update coding guide with platform-specific requirements

4. Use version control via setup_github_repo when available

content.text:
Deployed. To answer your question: the instructions are split across two places, and it's worth understanding which is which.

**`serverInstructions` in `internal/mcp/server.go`** — this is the platform MCP server's system prompt injected during `initialize`. It's what agents see when they connect to IAF directly. This is **platform code**, not coach code.

**`coding-guide` prompt in `internal/mcp/prompts/coding_guide.go`** — this is registered on the platform server. Also **platform code**.

**`internal/mcp/coach/coding_guide.go`** — this is the **coach copy** of the same prompt, for when the coach server is running standalone. I updated both to keep them in sync.

The problem is that right now there are two copies of `coding-guide` (platform + coach) that can drift. That's exactly the motivation for issue #58 (proxy) — once the proxy is configured, the platform will forward to the coach's copy and the platform's local one can be removed. But until `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform serves its own local copy, so I had to update both.

content.content:
platform.yaml
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: iaf-platform
  namespace: iaf-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: iaf-platform-binding
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: iaf-controller-role
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: iaf-platform
    namespace: iaf-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: iaf-sources
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
---
# IAF Controller
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-controller
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-controller
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-controller
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-controller
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: controller
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/controller"]
          env:
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
---
# IAF API Server (with MCP endpoint)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-apiserver
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-apiserver
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-apiserver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: apiserver
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/apiserver"]
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: IAF_API_PORT
              value: "8080"
            - name: IAF_API_TOKENS
              value: "iaf-dev-key"
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_DIR
              value: "/data/sources"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_URL
              value: "http://iaf-apiserver.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:8080"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sources
              mountPath: /data/sources
      volumes:
        - name: sources
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: iaf-sources
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  selector:
    app: iaf-apiserver
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`iaf.localhost`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: iaf-apiserver
          port: 8080

---
# IAF Controller
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-controller
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-controller
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-controller
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-controller
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: controller
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/controller"]
          env:
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
---
# IAF API Server (with MCP endpoint)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-apiserver
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-apiserver
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-apiserver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: apiserver
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/apiserver"]
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: IAF_API_PORT
              value: "8080"
            - name: IAF_API_TOKENS
              value: "iaf-dev-key"
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_DIR
              value: "/data/sources"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_URL
              value: "http://iaf-apiserver.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:8080"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sources
              mountPath: /data/sources
      volumes:
        - name: sources
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: iaf-sources
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  selector:
    app: iaf-apiserver
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`iaf.localhost`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: iaf-apiserver
          port: 8080

input.description: Check Dockerfile to understand build pattern

content.content:
FROM golang:1.25-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /apiserver ./cmd/apiserver
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /controller ./cmd/controller

FROM alpine:3.21
RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates
COPY --from=builder /apiserver /usr/local/bin/apiserver
COPY --from=builder /controller /usr/local/bin/controller

  replicas: 1

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: iaf-platform
  namespace: iaf-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: iaf-platform-binding
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: iaf-controller-role
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: iaf-platform
    namespace: iaf-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: iaf-sources
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
---
# IAF Controller
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-controller
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-controller
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-controller
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-controller
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: controller
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/controller"]
          env:
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
---
# IAF API Server (with MCP endpoint)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
  labels:
    app: iaf-apiserver
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: iaf-apiserver
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: iaf-apiserver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: iaf-platform
      containers:
        - name: apiserver
          image: iaf-platform:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Never
          command: ["/usr/local/bin/apiserver"]
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: IAF_API_PORT
              value: "8080"
            - name: IAF_API_TOKENS
              value: "iaf-dev-key"
            - name: IAF_CLUSTER_BUILDER
              value: "iaf-cluster-builder"
            - name: IAF_REGISTRY_PREFIX
              value: "registry.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:5000/iaf"
            - name: IAF_BASE_DOMAIN
              value: "localhost"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_DIR
              value: "/data/sources"
            - name: IAF_SOURCE_STORE_URL
              value: "http://iaf-apiserver.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local:8080"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sources
              mountPath: /data/sources
      volumes:
        - name: sources
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: iaf-sources
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  selector:
    app: iaf-apiserver
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: iaf-apiserver
  namespace: iaf-system
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`iaf.localhost`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: iaf-apiserver
          port: 8080
